commands.repair_reason: Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue after the latest code change addressed the prior security concern.; Cleared: The latest diff keeps sandbox/policy evaluation on the sandbox requester key and introduces no dependency, workflow, secret, or supply-chain changes. (sha=bd814d51024696eb0120fb0842b32ba1142cb349)
commands.review_summary:
The PR separates subagent completion ownership from sandbox policy, prefers requester bindings for completion announce routing, and adds focused regression coverage for the Telegram peer-session case.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main passes the sandbox/channel peer key into `sessions_spawn` ownership and checks child bindings before requester bindings. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue after the latest code change addressed the prior security concern.
